tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and imagine yourself as a shepherd, then reflect: Am I caring for those under my influence with genuine compassion?

two_step.reflect_question: In what areas of leadership or influence might I be 'feeding myself' instead of serving others?

options.response: Schedule time to mentor junior colleagues this week
Listen more actively and support family members' needs
Volunteer or contribute to a local support initiative

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a picture of a caring shepherd protecting and healing wounded sheep

reevaluation.question: How has this chapter challenged my understanding of leadership and care?
options: Leadership is about serving, not being served
True leaders heal and restore, not exploit
Compassion matters more than personal gain
Everyone has a responsibility to care for others

three_choice.question: Which profound insight resonates most about spiritual leadership?

choices.expansion: Power can corrupt leaders who prioritize personal gain over genuine care and nurturing.
God holds spiritual guides responsible for the spiritual and practical welfare of their community.
True leadership means healing, strengthening, and restoring those who are marginalized or wounded.

socratic_nudge.question: If spiritual leadership is meant to serve, how do we distinguish between genuine care and self-interested manipulation?

moral_slider.question: How do institutional power and spiritual authenticity coexist?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of spiritual leadership transformed?

thematic_summaries.title: Failed Leadership Accountability
thematic_summaries.summary: God condemns spiritual leaders who exploit their position for personal gain instead of caring for their congregations. These shepherds neglect the vulnerable and prioritize self-interest over pastoral responsibility.

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Restoration Promise
thematic_summaries.summary: Despite human leadership failures, God promises direct intervention to restore and shepherd His people. He will personally seek out, heal, and gather His scattered flock, demonstrating His unconditional covenant love.

reevaluation.question: How should spiritual accountability be understood in light of God's ultimate shepherding role?
options: Leaders are primarily responsible for spiritual care, not personal advancement
God ultimately judges and corrects leadership failures
Congregants share responsibility in holding leaders accountable
Divine grace supersedes human leadership shortcomings
